content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :    File                                          Date  :  November 7, 1983
                                                              M-83-251

From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Janis Shank McLean

Subject:  Reporting the Repayment of Travel Expenses


         Senator Ed Davis went to Canada to speak at a church and
view a Canadaire product. His campaign fund initially paid for
the trip. Later, the church reimbursed part of the trip and
Canadaire paid for the rest. How should this be reported?

    1.   The campaign committee reports the payments as
         reimbursements for expenses on its campaign statement;

    2.   On Senator Davis' SBI, he'll report a gift from
         Canadaire with the committee as an intermediary on
         Schedule G; and

    3.   He will not report the money received from the church
         because it is reimbursement for travel expenses and per
         diem received from a bona fide charitable
         organization. (Gov. Code Section 82039(b)(2).)

JSM:plh

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660             322-5901              322-6441                322-6444

November 28, 1983


The Honorable Ruben S. Ayala
State Capitol, Rm. 2082
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                          Re: Your Request for Advice, Our
                                              Advice No. A-83-252

Dear Senator Ayala:

     This letter is sent to confirm telephone advice I gave to
Ms. Rooks of your office concerning a situation described in
your letter of November 3, 1983.  You asked whether campaign
funds could be used to pay for your wife's travel expenses for a
trip to Taiwan.  You have been invited by the Government of
Taiwan to tour the country so that you may study and learn about
its agriculture and water projects.

     I advised Ms. Rooks that your question concerned the
application of Elections Code Section 12400, et seq., which
regulates the personal use of campaign funds.  That section
provides that campaign funds may be spent for travel by the
immediate family of an officeholder if the purpose of the
officeholder's travel bears a "reasonable relationship to
political, legislative or governmental purposes."  I referred
Ms. Rooks to the Attorney General's Office which is responsible
for the interpretation and enforcement of that statute.

     If campaign funds are used to pay your wife's travel
expenses, the payments should be reported on the appropriate
campaign statement as expenditures.  They need not be reported
as gifts on your Statement of Economic Interests.

     If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me at 916/322-5901.

                                          Sincerely,


                                          Diane Maura Fishburn
                                          Counsel, Legal Division

DMF:km

sections.question: His campaign fund initially paid for the trip. Later, the church reimbursed part of the trip and Canadaire paid for the rest. How should this be reported?
sections.conclusion: 1. The campaign committee reports the payments as reimbursements for expenses on its campaign statement; 2. On Senator Davis' SEI, he'll report a gift from Canadaire with the committee as an intermediary on Schedule Gj and 3. He will not report the money received from the church because it is reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received from a bona fide charitable organization. (Gov. Code Section 82039 (b) (2).)
sections.question_synthetic: How should Senator Davis report reimbursements for a trip to Canada where his campaign fund initially paid, but the church and Canadaire subsequently reimbursed portions of the expenses?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The campaign committee should report the reimbursements as expenses on its campaign statement; Senator Davis must report the Canadaire payment as a gift on his SEI with the committee as intermediary; and he need not report the church reimbursement as it qualifies as exempt charitable organization reimbursement under Government Code Section 82039(b)(2).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear question and three-part answer. Appears to be internal FPPC memorandum with practical advice on campaign finance reporting requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: His campaign fund initially paid for the trip. Later, the church reimbursed part of the trip and Canadaire paid for the rest. How should this be reported?

CONCLUSION: 1. The campaign committee reports the payments as reimbursements for expenses on its campaign statement; 2. On Senator Davis' SEI, he'll report a gift from Canadaire with the committee as an intermediary on Schedule Gj and 3. He will not report the money received from the church because it is reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received from a bona fide charitable organization. (Gov. Code Section 82039 (b) (2).)

You asked whether campaign funds could be used to pay for your wife's travel expenses for a trip to Taiwan. You have been invited by the Government of Taiwan to tour the country so that you may study and learn about its agriculture and water projects. I advised Ms. Rooks that your question concerned the application of Elections Code Section 12400, et seq., which regulates the personal use of campaign funds. That section provides that campaign funds may be spent for travel by the immediate family of an officeholder if the purpose of the officeholder's travel bears a "reasonable relationship to political, legislative or governmental purposes." embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum advising on the proper reporting of travel expense reimbursements for Senator Ed Davis when multiple parties (church and Canadaire) repaid portions of a trip initially funded by his campaign committee.
